issues.description: Component grouping folder uses PascalCase 'ProductDetail' instead of kebab-case 'product-detail'. Both 'ProductDetail/' and 'product-detail/' coexist with identical contents, creating a duplicate directory structure.
issues.rule: Folders should use kebab-case per naming-conventions.md (Component folders: PascalCase only when used as a grouping for a single named component). The convention doc states feature folders use kebab-case.
issues.fix: Consolidate to a single folder. Prefer 'product-detail/' (kebab-case) for component grouping directories, and update all imports accordingly.

issues.description: Component grouping folder uses PascalCase 'InlineEditor' instead of kebab-case 'inline-editor'. Both 'InlineEditor/' and 'inline-editor/' coexist with near-identical contents, creating a duplicate directory.
issues.rule: Feature/grouping folders should use kebab-case per naming-conventions.md.
issues.fix: Consolidate to 'inline-editor/' (kebab-case) and update all imports. Remove the PascalCase duplicate.

issues.description: Component grouping folder uses PascalCase 'Delegates' instead of kebab-case 'delegates'.
issues.rule: Feature/grouping folders should use kebab-case per naming-conventions.md.
issues.fix: Rename to 'delegates/' and update all imports.

issues.description: Sub-grouping folder uses PascalCase 'Sections' instead of kebab-case 'sections'. The parallel 'product-detail/sections/' folder uses the correct casing.
issues.fix: Rename to 'sections/' (already correct in the kebab-case counterpart).

issues.description: Type definition file uses camelCase 'sectionItemTypes.ts'. Type files should use PascalCase per naming-conventions.md (Types/Interfaces: PascalCase → 'UserTypes.ts'). The PascalCase counterpart 'SectionItemTypes.ts' exists in the InlineEditor/ directory.
issues.rule: Types files must use PascalCase: 'SectionItemTypes.ts' not 'sectionItemTypes.ts'.
issues.fix: Rename to 'SectionItemTypes.ts' after consolidating the two duplicate folders.

issues.description: Hook files (useProducts.ts, useProductForm.ts, useLangToggle.ts, useProductMutations.ts, useAutoResize.ts) exist directly in 'application/' AND are duplicated inside 'application/hooks/'. The convention is to place hooks inside 'hooks/' subdirectory.
issues.rule: Hooks should be placed in the 'application/hooks/' subdirectory.
issues.fix: Remove the duplicates at the 'application/' root level, keeping only the 'application/hooks/' versions. Update all imports.

issues.description: Hook file 'useAuditLog.ts' exists directly in 'application/' AND is duplicated inside 'application/hooks/'. Similarly in store: 'useGridCols.ts', 'useStoreProducts.ts' exist in 'application/' and 'application/hooks/'.
issues.rule: Hooks should be placed in the 'application/hooks/' subdirectory.
issues.fix: Remove duplicates at the 'application/' root level. Ensure 'useFlyToCart' and 'useSellerProfiles' in store/cart are also moved to a 'hooks/' subfolder or consolidated.

issues.description: File is named 'PaymentMethodTypes.ts' (PascalCase). The naming convention for types files is PascalCase so this is technically correct. However, it lives in the 'domain/' layer alongside 'types.ts', which means there are two type files in the same domain folder with different naming patterns.
issues.rule: Types/Interfaces: PascalCase — this file is compliant. Informational only.
issues.fix: Consider consolidating into a single 'types.ts' file or ensuring consistent grouping strategy across domain type files.

issues.description: Local module-level constants (ORDER_READ_PERMISSIONS, CHECKOUT_PERMISSIONS, SALES_PERMISSIONS, SELLER_PAYMENT_METHOD_PERMISSIONS, ASSIGNED_PERMISSIONS, INACTIVE_LINK_CLASS) use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. These are not exported globals but file-level constants used only in this component. Per naming-conventions.md, local constants should use camelCase.
issues.fix: Rename to camelCase: orderReadPermissions, checkoutPermissions, salesPermissions, sellerPaymentMethodPermissions, assignedPermissions, inactiveLinkClass.

patterns.description: Multiple apps show a migration pattern where PascalCase component folders (InlineEditor, ProductDetail) were being renamed to kebab-case counterparts (inline-editor, product-detail), but both old and new versions still coexist. This suggests an incomplete migration.
patterns.recommendation: Complete the migration: remove PascalCase folders after updating all imports to the kebab-case versions.

patterns.description: Several features (audit in admin, products in store/studio, cart in store) have hooks placed both directly in 'application/' and in 'application/hooks/', suggesting an ongoing structural migration.
patterns.recommendation: Standardize: all hooks should live in 'application/hooks/', remove any duplicates at the 'application/' root.

patterns.description: File naming for components is consistently PascalCase across all apps. Hook files uniformly use the 'use' prefix with camelCase. Props interfaces consistently use the 'ComponentNameProps' suffix pattern. Constants in domain/constants.ts files use correct SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. Overall naming discipline is high.
patterns.recommendation: Continue the current discipline. Address the two migration-in-progress patterns noted above.
